summary: This cluster of research focuses on the taxonomic revision of the diatom genus *Amphora sensu lato*, integrating molecular phylogenetics with detailed morphological analysis to resolve species boundaries and evolutionary relationships. A key finding was the distinction of the subgenus *Halamphora Cleve* as a separate lineage, supporting its reclassification, and the formal identification of *Amphora terroris* as a new species. The studies underscore the critical role of morphological data in validating molecular-based phylogenies for this globally distributed group, which inhabits diverse marine and estuarine environments.
